662.reason: [This reports the error at the current token position, but for error type 'i' (missing semicolon), the specification requires reporting the line number of the last non-terminal before the semicolon, not the current lookahead token position]

795.reason: [When a semicolon is missing, this reports the error at curPos (which points to the token after the last parsed component), but should report at the line of the last successfully parsed non-terminal (e.g., the last ConstDef)]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a semicolon is missing, this reports the error at curPos (which points to the token after the last parsed component), but should report at the line of the last successfully parsed non-terminal (e.g., the last ConstDef)]

661.code: Error error = new Error(errorType, token.getTokenRow(), token.getTokenCol());
661.reason: [The error line number should correspond to the last non-terminal component before the missing symbol, not the current token position. For error types i, j, k, the specification requires using the line of the last meaningful non-terminal, but this uses curPos which may point to a subsequent token or wrong location.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The error line number should correspond to the last non-terminal component before the missing symbol, not the current token position. For error types i, j, k, the specification requires using the line of the last meaningful non-terminal, but this uses curPos which may point to a subsequent token or wrong location.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The error line number should correspond to the line where the last non-terminal before the missing symbol is located, but this uses the current token position which may not be correct for error types i, j, k as specified in the problem description]

796.reason: [When a semicolon is missing, the parser reports error 'i' but doesn't consume any token to recover, causing subsequent parsing to be misaligned. The parser should attempt recovery by treating the next available token as a virtual semicolon or skip to a synchronization point.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [When a semicolon is missing, the parser reports error 'i' but doesn't consume any token to recover, causing subsequent parsing to be misaligned. The parser should attempt recovery by treating the next available token as a virtual semicolon or skip to a synchronization point.]

663.reason: [This error handling uses curPos which points to the last successfully parsed token, but for missing semicolon errors (type 'i'), the specification requires reporting the line number of the last non-terminal before the semicolon. However, in some contexts like Stmt parsing, curPos might not correctly point to the last meaningful token of the statement when the semicolon is missing, leading to incorrect line number reporting.]
